channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Eager visitors flock to see spectacular lava fountaining from Kilauea eruption in Hawaii
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — People were flocking to Hawaii Volcanoes National Park on Wednesday to get a glimpse of fountaining lava. The eruption that began Dec. 23 in a crater at the summit of Kilauea volcano has paused periodically.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida grandmother who left infant in hot car found not guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A Florida grandmother was acquitted of manslaughter in the death of her 7-month-old granddaughter, but was convicted on a lesser charge.
item.dc:creator: Staci DaSilva, WGN 9

item.title: South Carolina man dies of Legionnaires' disease after using bacteria-laden hot tub, lawsuit claims
item.description: Two weeks after using a hot tub in his South Carolina home, 72-year-old Wayn Van Der Plaats was fighting for his life, his body racked by a deadly bacteria that causes Legionnaires' disease.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden warns of dangers of 'oligarchy' of ultra-rich, 'tech-industrial complex' running country
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver his farewell address to the nation on Wednesday, days before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Couple heartbroken after Eaton Fire destroys home on their 62nd wedding anniversary
item.description: Herman and Juanita Landry are two of the many thousands who lost everything in the fire, which has claimed the lives of 16 people, destroyed over 7,000 structures.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: Man gets 2 years in US prison for aiding pushy effort to get ex-official to return to China
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man convicted in the first trial highlighting U.S. claims that China harasses its critics overseas was sentenced Wednesday to two years in prison for his role in a creepy campaign to get a former official to return to his homeland.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — More than a week into what could be the costliest wildfires in U.S. history, weary and anxious Los Angeles-area residents were hoping for a favorable shift in the winds expected Thursday and an end to the constant fear that their lives might be further upended.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, Fox 5

item.title: Grandma who went viral after Los Angeles wildfire engulfed her home optimistic about starting over at 90
item.description: A nearly 90-year-old shares her perspective on moving forward and simplifying her life after losing everything in the devastating Eaton Fires in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NY federal judge denies bail for two real estate brokers and their brother in sex trafficking case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two luxury real estate brokers and their brother accused of raping dozens of women over two decades will remain in jail to await their trial on sex trafficking charges, a federal judge in New York City ruled Wednesday. Judge Valerie E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MacDill service member found dead at off-base home in Tampa
item.description: Tech. Sgt. Felicia Singleton, a U.S. Air Force airman from the 6th Security Forces Squadron, was found by law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson removes Mike Turner as Intelligence Committee chair
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will not make Rep. Mike Turner (R-Ohio) chair of the House Intelligence Committee in the 119th Congress, a source familiar with the decision confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: A majority of the justices suggested the concept of an age-verification requirement could survive First Amendment scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, NewsNation

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg backs Carrie Underwood inauguration performance
item.description: The comments about Underwood’s inauguration performance are notable coming from Goldberg, who is commonly a harsh critic of Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Whistleblower says UFO retrieval program exists: NewsNation exclusive
item.description: In an exclusive interview with NewsNation, whistleblower Jake Barber believes he recovered alien technology.
item.dc:creator: Ross Coulthart, NewsNation

item.title: American woman on Paris-to-Boston flight dies in midair
item.description: A passenger died on board during an Air France flight from Paris to Boston on Tuesday despite life-saving efforts performed by a doctor on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alabama inmate asks court to block nitrogen gas execution
item.description: Attorneys for an Alabama inmate facing execution by nitrogen gas are asking a federal judge to block the upcoming execution.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: California Gov. Newsom slammed for 'sideshow' as he defends state's response
item.description: A California lawmaker is urging leaders to learn from their mistakes from previous California wildfires and unite to come up with a solution to wildfire prevention.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles fire captain loses home to Eaton Fire
item.description: A man tasked with protecting the Los Angeles area from raging wildfires now ranks among those who have lost their homes and everything inside.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, Fox 5

item.title: Biden hails ceasefire; says American hostages to be freed in initial phase
item.description: President Biden hailed a ceasefire agreement between Hamas and Israel on Wednesday that would include the release of American hostages, marking a major diplomatic win in a foreign conflict that had come to define his final year in office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: About 2 in 10 Americans back Hegseth as Trump's Defense secretary: Survey
item.description: In the poll from The Associated Press-NORC Research Center, 19 percent of respondents said that they “strongly approve” or “somewhat approve” the selection of Hegseth by Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Palisades Fire firefighters taking part in first-of-its-kind cancer study
item.description: The study comes after the International Agency for Research on Cancer officially classified firefighting as a carcinogenic profession.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Officials tout Gaza ceasefire, hostage deal as Israel says details still in flux
item.description: Israel and Hamas agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Flags ordered to fly at full-staff on Inauguration Day in multiple states
item.description: GOP governors of at least eight states ordered flags to be flown at full-staff on Inauguration Day, bucking tradition by raising flags before the end of a customary 30-day mourning period following the death of former President Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: US in plea talks with Mexican cartel kingpin Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. prosecutors said Wednesday that they're discussing a potential plea deal with Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, the long-elusive Mexican drug lord who was arrested last summer and whose son could testify against him if he goes to trial. Assistant U.S.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wildfires latest: A final round of dangerous fire weather and dry conditions is in the forecast
item.description: Millions of Southern Californians were on edge as winds began picking up during a final round of dangerous fire weather forecast for the region Wednesday where two massive blazes have killed at least 25 and destroyed thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from snacks, medicine
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer, a potential 2028 candidate, wants to find common ground with Trump
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer wants a fresh start with President-elect Donald Trump but also warns that she won't back down over tariffs that she believes would hurt the auto industry in her state.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FDA proposes sweeping limits on nicotine in cigarettes
item.description: It's an 11th-hour plan from the Biden administration that will take years to go into effect, if it happens at all.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass posed for photos at a cocktail party as Palisades Fire exploded
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass is at the center of controversy after social media photos showed her posing at a cocktail party in Ghana as the Palisades Fire exploded.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Few US adults confident Justice Department and FBI will act fairly under Trump, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: As President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, prepares for questioning from senators on Wednesday, only about 2 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fire tornadoes pose a threat in California. A fire lab shows how they work
item.description: The National Weather Service's warning about the “particularly dangerous situation” in which any new fire could explode in size in the Los Angeles area didn’t mention fire tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: Holly Ramer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dire fire warning for LA area pushed back as winds ease
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Easing winds delivered a brief but much-needed reprieve to firefighters Tuesday as they battled two massive blazes burning in the Los Angeles area, and the National Weather Service pushed back its unusually dire warning of critical fire weather until early the following day.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9
